front: Model vs app - what is the difference?
back: The model (LLM) is the raw engine: text in, text out. The app is the car around it: history, memory, files, voice, tools, and UI.

front: What is the company-model-app chain for OpenAI?
back: OpenAI (company) builds GPT-5 (model), which powers ChatGPT (app).

front: What is the company-model-app chain for Anthropic?
back: Anthropic (company) builds Claude models like Claude Opus, which power the Claude app and Claude Code.

front: Name four things the app layer adds on top of the model
back: Chat history, saved memory, file uploads, voice, tools (like web search), and the user interface.

front: Why can a chatbot change behavior without a new model?
back: The app layer changed - a different system prompt, memory logic, or tools. The experience is model + app, not just the model.

front: Claude Opus vs Sonnet vs Haiku
back: One model family in three sizes: Opus (most capable), Sonnet (balanced), Haiku (fastest and cheapest).

front: Which layer of the AI stack do YOU control?
back: The prompt. You cannot change the training or the app, but a sharper prompt can dramatically improve results from the same model.
